title: 4.1 Cell Cycle
description: The cell cycle stages, interphase, mitosis phase, and cancer.

questions.question: What is a cell cycle?
options: A process by which a cell loses its genetic material
A sequence of events in the parent cell distributing genetic materials to form daughter cells
The movement of chromosomes during cell division only
The growth phase of a cell before it dies
questions.correctAnswer: A sequence of events in the parent cell distributing genetic materials to form daughter cells

questions.question: What are the two main divisions of the cell cycle?
Into how many phases is interphase divided?
What are the three phases of interphase?
What is the first stage of interphase called?
Which phase of interphase is also known as the first gap?
During the G1 phase, what do cells accumulate as building blocks?
How would you describe the metabolic activity of cells during G1 phase?
options: Cells are only active in DNA replication

questions.question: During G1 phase, cells store sufficient energy reserves to complete the task of what?
options: Replicating each chromosome in the nucleus
questions.correctAnswer: Replicating each chromosome in the nucleus

questions.question: What is the main event of the S phase?
What does the S in S phase stand for?
What is the stage of DNA replication called?
Which phase of interphase is also known as the second gap?
During the G2 phase, the cell replenishes its energy stores and synthesizes proteins necessary for what?
During which phase of interphase are some cell organelles duplicated?
During which phase does the cell make final preparations before entering the mitotic phase?
What does cell division include?
options: Nuclear division followed by cytoplasmic division
DNA replication followed by protein synthesis
questions.correctAnswer: Nuclear division followed by cytoplasmic division

questions.question: What is the scientific term for nuclear division?
The M-stage in the cell cycle is also known as what?
What is the term for cytoplasmic division?
Cell division ultimately results in how many daughter cells?
options: One daughter cell and one parent cell

questions.question: What happens to each daughter cell after the cell cycle completes?
options: It grows and starts the cycle the mother cell underwent
It fuses with another daughter cell
questions.correctAnswer: It grows and starts the cycle the mother cell underwent

questions.question: What enables the cell cycle to follow a regular pattern?
What is the term used when cells excessively divide due to failure of the regulatory system?
Cancer occurs as a result of failure in what process?
When can cancer start according to the textbook?
options: When the controlling factors over cell division and cell growth fail
When DNA replication occurs too slowly
When the cell membrane breaks down
questions.correctAnswer: When the controlling factors over cell division and cell growth fail

questions.question: Cancer occurs in the absence of what factors?
What allows a cancer cell to divide continuously at very high density?
Cancer cells divide at high density at the expense of what?
Which of the following is a characteristic problem of cancer cells?
options: They communicate better with healthy cells
They might not be able to communicate with healthy cells
They produce more energy than normal cells
They divide more slowly than normal cells
questions.correctAnswer: They might not be able to communicate with healthy cells

questions.question: What is one problem cancer cells may have regarding normal cell functions?
options: They may perform normal functions better than healthy cells
They may not be able to carry out normal cell functions
They always carry out normal cell functions
They only carry out reproductive functions
questions.correctAnswer: They may not be able to carry out normal cell functions

questions.question: Why are cancer cells more likely to travel and spread to other parts of the body?
options: They have a stronger cell membrane
They may not securely anchor themselves like other cells
They are much smaller than normal cells
questions.correctAnswer: They may not securely anchor themselves like other cells

questions.question: What makes cancer cells different from normal cells in terms of cell cycle control?
options: They respond normally to the cell cycle controlling system
They do not respond normally to the cell cycle controlling system
They have no cell cycle at all
They only respond to external signals
questions.correctAnswer: They do not respond normally to the cell cycle controlling system

questions.question: Cancer cells divide in what manner?
options: An orderly fashion like normal cells

questions.question: What may cancer cells do to other tissues of the body?
